Uses of Class
org.moeaframework.util.tree.Node

Packages that use Node
Package
Description
org.moeaframework.core.operator.program
Collection of operators for expression trees for use with Genetic Programming (GP).
org.moeaframework.core.variable
Defines the various decision variables that may be used.
org.moeaframework.util.tree
Classes for representing, building and evaluating expression trees for use in Genetic Programming (GP).

  • Uses of Node in org.moeaframework.core.operator.program

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected void
    PointMutation.mutate(Node node, Rules rules)
    Applies point mutation to the specified node.

  • Uses of Node in org.moeaframework.util.tree

    Subclasses of Node in org.moeaframework.util.tree
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Abs
    The node for calculating the absolute value of a number.
    class 
    Acos
    The node for calculating the arc cosine of a number.
    class 
    Acosh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic arc cosine of a number.
    class 
    Add
    The node for adding two numbers.
    class 
    And
    The node for calculating the logical AND of two boolean values.
    class 
    Asin
    The node for calculating the arc sine of a number.
    class 
    Asinh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic arc sine of a number.
    class 
    Atan
    The node for calculating the arc tangent of a number.
    class 
    Atanh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic arc tangent of a number.
    class 
    Call
    The node for calling a named function.
    class 
    Ceil
    The node for calculating the smallest integer value that is greater than or equal to a number.
    class 
    Constant
    The node for defining a constant value.
    class 
    Cos
    The node for calculating the trigonometric cosine of an angle specified in radians.
    class 
    Cosh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic cosine of a number.
    class 
    Define
    The node for defining a callable, named function.
    class 
    Divide
    The node for dividing two numbers.
    class 
    Equals
    The node for comparing two numbers for equality.
    class 
    Exp
    The node for calculating the result of Euler's number e raised to the power of a number.
    class 
    Floor
    The node for calculating the largest integer value that is less than or equal to a number.
    class 
    For
    The node for executing an expression for a given number of iterations.
    class 
    Get
    The node for reading the value stored in a named variable within the current scope.
    class 
    GreaterThan
    The node for comparing if one number is greater than another.
    class 
    GreaterThanOrEqual
    The node for comparing if a number is greater than or equal to another.
    class 
    IfElse
    The node for executing one of two expressions depending on the result of a boolean expression.
    class 
    Lambda
    The node for defining an immutable, anonymous function.
    class 
    LessThan
    The node for comparing if a number is less than another.
    class 
    LessThanOrEqual
    The node for comparing if a number is less than or equal to another.
    class 
    Log
    The node for calculating the natural logarithm of a number.
    class 
    Log10
    The node for calculating the base-10 logarithm of a number.
    class 
    Max
    The node for calculating the maximum value of the two arguments.
    class 
    Min
    The node for calculating the minimum value of the two arguments.
    class 
    Modulus
    The node for calculating the modulus, or remainder, of two numbers.
    class 
    Multiply
    The node for multiplying two numbers.
    class 
    NOP
    The node for defining an empty expression.
    class 
    Not
    The node for calculating the logical NOT of a boolean value.
    class 
    Or
    The node for calculating the logical OR of two boolean values.
    class 
    Power
    The node for calculating the power of a base number and exponent.
    class 
    Round
    The node for rounding a number to the nearest integer.
    class 
    Sequence
    The node for executing two or more expressions in sequence.
    class 
    Set
    The node for assigning the value of a named variable within the current scope.
    class 
    Sign
    The node for calculating the sign of a number.
    class 
    Sin
    The node for calculating the trigonometric sine of an angle specified in radians.
    class 
    Sinh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic sine of a number.
    class 
    Square
    The node for calculating the square of a number.
    class 
    SquareRoot
    The node for calculating the square root of a number.
    class 
    Subtract
    The node for subtracting two numbers.
    class 
    Tan
    The node for calculating the trigonometric tangent of an angle specified in radians.
    class 
    Tanh
    The node for calculating the hyperbolic tangent of a number.
    class 
    Truncate
    The node for truncating, or bounding, a number within a range.
    class 
    While
    The node for repeatedly executing an expression while a condition, a boolean expression, remains true.
    Methods in org.moeaframework.util.tree that return Node
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeFull(Class<?> type, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given return type using the full initialization method.
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeFull(Node node, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given scaffolding using the full initialization method.
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeGrow(Class<?> type, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given return type using the grow initialization method.
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeGrow(Node node, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given scaffolding using the grow initialization method.
    Node
    Call.copyNode()
     
    Node
    Define.copyNode()
     
    Node
    Lambda.copyNode()
     
    abstract Node
    Node.copyNode()
    Returns a copy of this node, but without any children or parents assigned.
    Node
    Node.copyTree()
    Returns a copy of this node including copies of all its arguments (child nodes).
    Node
    Node.getArgument(int index)
    Returns the argument (child node) at the specified index.
    Node
    Node.getFunctionAt(int index)
    Returns the function (non-terminal) node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node.
    Node
    Node.getFunctionAt(Class<?> type, int index)
    Returns the function (non-terminal) node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node, counting only nodes that match the given return type.
    Node
    Node.getNodeAt(int index)
    Returns the node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node.
    protected Node
    Node.getNodeAt(Class<?> type, boolean includeFunctions, boolean includeTerminals, int index)
    Returns the node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node.
    Node
    Node.getNodeAt(Class<?> type, int index)
    Returns the node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node, counting only nodes that match the given return type.
    Node
    Node.getParent()
    Returns the parent of this node; or null if this node has no parent.
    Node
    Rules.getScaffolding()
    Returns the program scaffolding; or null if the program has no defined scaffolding.
    Node
    Node.getTerminalAt(int index)
    Returns the terminal node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node.
    Node
    Node.getTerminalAt(Class<?> type, int index)
    Returns the terminal node at the specified index in the subtree rooted at this node, counting only nodes that match the given return type.
    Node
    Node.setArgument(int index, Node expression)
    Sets the argument (child node) at the specified index.
    Methods in org.moeaframework.util.tree that return types with arguments of type Node
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    List<Node>
    Rules.getAvailableNodes()
    Returns the list of all nodes which can appear in programs produced using these rules.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableCrossoverNodes(Node node, Class<?> type)
    Returns the list of all nodes in the tree rooted at the specified node with the given return type.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableFunctions(Class<?> type)
    Returns the list of all available function (non-terminal) nodes with the given return type.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableMutations(Node node)
    Returns the list of all available mutations to the given node.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableNodes(Class<?> type)
    Returns the list of all available nodes with the given return type.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableTerminals(Class<?> type)
    Returns the list of all available terminal nodes with the given return type.
    Methods in org.moeaframework.util.tree with parameters of type Node
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    Rules.add(Node node)
    Adds a node that can appear in programs produced using these rules.
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeFull(Node node, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given scaffolding using the full initialization method.
    Node
    Rules.buildTreeGrow(Node node, int depth)
    Generates an expression tree with the given scaffolding using the grow initialization method.
    protected boolean
    Rules.isMutationCompatible(Node original, Node mutation)
    Returns true if the original node can be replaced via point mutation with the given mutation node and satisfy type safety; false otherwise.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableCrossoverNodes(Node node, Class<?> type)
    Returns the list of all nodes in the tree rooted at the specified node with the given return type.
    List<Node>
    Rules.listAvailableMutations(Node node)
    Returns the list of all available mutations to the given node.
    Node
    Node.setArgument(int index, Node expression)
    Sets the argument (child node) at the specified index.
    void
    Rules.setScaffolding(Node scaffolding)
    Sets the program scaffolding.
    Constructors in org.moeaframework.util.tree with parameters of type Node
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    Lambda(Node node)
    Constructs a new node for defining an immutable, anonymous function with no arguments.
     
    Lambda(Node node, String[] variableNames, Class<?>[] variableTypes)
    Constructs a new node for defining an immutable, anonymous function with a user-defined number of arguments.
     
    Lambda(Node node, String variableName, Class<?> variableType)
    Constructs a new node for defining an immutable, anonymous function with one argument.
     
    Lambda(Node node, String name1, Class<?> type1, String name2, Class<?> type2)
    Constructs a new node for defining an immutable, anonymous function with two arguments.
